response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work, FOSS4G speaker) and real low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers. However, that systems work was in DLang rather than the required modern C++ or Rust; his C/C++ experience is brief and dated (~7 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015), and recent roles have been TypeScript/Node.js product work. He has no direct routing, navigation, or map-tiling pipeline experience, making him an adjacent rather than direct fit for a routing-tiles role.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform experience as founder of GISCollective and Green Map GIS applications
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, broad full-stack adaptability
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (short 2014-2015 stint) and no Rust
No routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline background
Recent years focused on TypeScript/Node product work, away from systems programming
